outcome: Independent walking at end of intervention phase, all electromechanical devices used (critical outcome)
source_quote: FAC at T2: EG FAC 4=11, FAC 5=0 (11/38 independent); CG FAC 4=11, FAC 5=3 (14/37 independent). 29% and 8% of subjects were independent ambulators who could walk freely only on level surfaces in the EG and the CG, respectively.

outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: 10MWT baseline values reported (EG 0.25 +/- 0.19, CG 0.20 +/- 0.26 m/s) but post-intervention (T2) means and SDs for 10MWT not explicitly reported in the paper. Only ANOVA time effect p<0.0001 and time*group p=0.05 reported.

outcome: Walking capacity (metres walked in 6 minutes) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: The 6MWT increased from 48.60 +/- 42.39 m (T1) to 139.24 +/- 104.7 m (T2) in the EG and from 44.29 +/- 59.15 m (T1) to 149.43 +/- 130.15 m (T2) in the CG.

outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: 10MWT change scores: Mean Loko 0.24 +/- 0.24, Mean CGT 0.23 +/- 0.29 m/s. The Lokomat group went from 0.02m/s to 0.26m/s and the CGT group went from 0.17m/s to 0.40m/s.

outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: 10MWT (seconds) post-test: Robot A 15.5 +/- 3.6, Robot B 27.1 +/- 7.6, Robot C 28.6 +/- 8.5, Non-Robot 21.6 +/- 4.4. Note: values are in seconds for 10m walk, not m/s. Robot A (30% BWS) used as experimental group as it showed greatest improvement.
